subject_tesim: Santa Monica State Beach Park (Santa Monica, Calif.)
description_tesim: For original Santa Monica publicity brochure see image ark no. 21198/zz002d7bsx.

A mock-up advertisement with brochure for the City of Santa Monica. At right, a handwritten sign with two arrows points toward the brochure, here partially photographed. Text reads: "Help to Publicize Your own City! Step into the Publicity Dept. at the City Hall and Get one of these New 4-Page Folders On Santa Monica. No Extra Postage Required to include one in your out-of-Town Letters." The brochure includes an image of Santa Monica Beach with the Breakers Beach Club, Edgewater Beach Club, and Hotel Casa Del Mar, and an image of palm trees atop a Palisades cliff overlooking the bay. Brochure text reads: "Santa Monica, California. The City with a Standardized Climate. 64 [degrees] Average Year-round Temperature. Only 20 degrees difference between the High and Low Temperatures for the year..." A map showing points of interest between Downtown Los Angeles and Santa Monica is partially captured at left.
combined_subject_ssim: Hotel Casa del Mar (Santa Monica, Calif.)
Santa Monica State Beach Park (Santa Monica, Calif.)

title_tesim: "Circus Days" float in the Tournament of Roses Parade, Pasadena, 1949
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: Pasadena Rose Parade, 1949?
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: Photograph of the "Circus Days" float, entered by the Standard Oil Co. of California, in the Tournament of Roses Parade (Pasadena Rose Parade). The float is constructed in several sections and is headed by a floral covered elephant ridden by a live circus girl. The words "Circus Days" are spelled out in flowers on the platform. The elephant is followed by two elaborate cage wagons and a floral giraffe standing on a platform with imitation trees and shrubs. An additional float is viewed in the background. Banners with rose designs hang above the Pasadena street alongside banners with iconic illustrations and text reading "Childhood Memories." Throngs of spectators line the parade route that is populated by businesses - including a Bank of America, Save Way Drug, and Mobil gas station (signs out of view in this image). One building advertises "Real Estate" above its portico.
This photograph is described in the article, "Fantasy in Rose Parade Thrills More Than Million: Childhood Memories Stirred Rose Tournament," Los Angeles Times, 2 Jan. 1949: 1.

title_tesim: "First Formal" float, entered by Pasadena city schools in the Tournament of Roses Parade, Pasadena, 1949

funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: This float is described in the article, "Rose Parade Floats Rich in Memories," Los Angeles Times, 3 Jan. 1949: A9.

Photograph of the "First Formal" float, entered by Pasadena city schools in the Tournament of Roses Parade (Pasadena Rose Parade). The float carries five young women wearing formal dresses and holding bouquets of flowers. One young woman waves to the crowds from her seat at the top of the float. Words spelled out in flowers around the float read: "First Party Formal" and "Pasadena Schools..." Throngs of spectators are captured  seated and standing along a Pasadena street. Signs for businesses lining the street include: "Hardware," "Bank of America," "Save Way Drugs" [sign for this business also advertises Coca Cola], "Mobil gas," "...Wright Co." and "Real Estate."

Santa Monica City Hall (Santa Monica, Calif.)
Native Sons of the Golden West
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: Photograph of Congressman John F. Dockweiler (wearing a caballero outfit with hat, embroidered short jacket, cravat at neck and sash around waist), posed with author and columnist Irwin S. Cobb (center), and Postmaster Philip T. Hill in front of a covered carriage with an unidentified female seated inside. A man and boy, wearing western attire, are viewed in the driver’s seat. Posted near the boy is a sign reading, “Irvin S. Cobb, Congressmen Doc[kweiler], Postmaster.” Spectators are viewed in the background looking toward the camera. Photographed during the California Admission Day parade in front of City Hall, Santa Monica.
Santa Monica City Hall was located at 4th Street and Oregon Avenue (later changed to Santa Monica Boulevard) from 1903 to 1938.

In 1937, Santa Monica hosted the California Admission Day celebration and convention of the Native Sons and Daughters of the Golden West. The three-day event (held September 8-10), commemorated the 87th anniversary of California’s admission to the Union. The Los Angeles Times published several articles between September 7th and 10th that described planned festivities, but a more detailed account of the celebration can be found in The Grizzly Bear, a magazine published by the Native Sons and Daughters of the Golden West (1905-1954). The article is titled, "Happy Memories of Admission Day," The Grizzly Bear, 1 Oct. 1937: 3.
combined_subject_ssim: Native Daughters of the Golden West
Santa Monica City Hall (Santa Monica, Calif.)
Native Sons of the Golden West

description_tesim: Photograph of Congressman John F. Dockweiler (wearing a hat and embroidered jacket) seated in a covered carriage next to author and columnist Irvin S. Cobb. Postmaster Philip T. Hill and an unidentified woman are seated opposite. Photographed during the California Admission Day parade in Santa Monica.

In 1937, Santa Monica hosted the California Admission Day celebration and convention of the Native Sons and Daughters of the Golden West. The three-day event (held September 8-10), commemorated the 87th anniversary of California’s admission to the Union. The Los Angeles Times published several articles between September 7th and 10th that described planned festivities, but a more detailed account of the celebration can be found in The Grizzly Bear, a magazine published by the Native Sons and Daughters of the Golden West (1905-1954). The article is titled, "Happy Memories of Admission Day," The Grizzly Bear, 1 Oct. 1937: 3.
combined_subject_ssim: Native Daughters of the Golden West
Native Sons of the Golden West

description_tesim: This dedication ceremony marked the official start of Admissions Day festivities and is noted in the article, "Happy Memories of Admission Day," The Grizzly Bear, 1 Oct. 1937: 3.

In 1937, Santa Monica hosted the Admission Day celebration, a three-day festival (September 8-10) observing California's admission to the Union. The celebration was presented by the Native Sons and Native Daughters of the Golden West.

Photograph of actor Charles Grapewin speaking at the Louis B. Mayer Memorial Bench dedication at Palisades Park. The bench was dedicated by the Native Sons of the Golden West. Eldred L. Meyer, Grand President of the Native Sons, stands in the background at ground level (wearing his regalia). The Georgian Hotel (left, 1415 Ocean Avenue) and Windermere Hotel are visible in the background.

title_tesim: Colonel Robert A. Bringham saluting along with two other veterans at the Armistice Day parade, Santa Monica, 1938
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: 2876. Armistice Day 1938, Santa Monica, also Soldiers Home. Col. RA Bringham Mgr, John F. Eaton, Veteran (age 92) of John A Martin Post GAR. Parade of Santa Monica Streets.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: Col. Robert A. (Amedee) Bringham, manager of the Sawtelle Veterans Home (National Home for Disabled Volunteer Soldiers, Pacific Branch), photographed saluting with two other veterans at the Armistice Day parade in Santa Monica. A flag viewed in the background includes text that reads: "Unknown Soldiers Post 875, West Los Angeles, California, [Mustered] in [Jan.] 3rd...Veterans of [Foreign Wars of United States]." The group's emblem (a Cross of Malta with the organization's name encircling an eagle and flag topped with thirteen stars) is placed at center. A second flag reads, in part, "Sawtelle...Soldiers...American" and includes an emblem at center. Additional men can be seen in the background talking and walking about. In the foreground, two microphones are placed in front of what is probably the parade review stand.

Santa Monica's Armistice Day parade is noted in the article, "Southland Cities Ready for Patriotic Services: Parade, School Pageants and Assemblies to Feature Armistice Observances Today," Los Angeles Times, 11 Nov. 1938.

title_tesim: Cargo truck carrying a military brass band during the Armistice Day parade, Santa Monica, 1938
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: 2876. Armistice Day 1938, Santa Monica, also Soldiers Home. Col. RA Bringham Mgr, John F. Eaton, Veteran (age 92) of John A Martin Post GAR. Parade of Santa Monica Streets.

description_tesim: Photograph of a cargo truck carrying a military brass band during the Santa Monica Armistice Day parade. The men wear dress uniforms and helmets with insignias on the front. They read from sheet music mounted on their instruments. Printed on the passenger-side of the truck: "63rd CA. Btry. A." Printed on the door is the Unit's Coat of Arms - a shield with three winged projectiles, one and two counterchanged, above a scroll reading, "Amor Patriae" ("Love of Country"). Printed on the hood: "W-4772." In addition, a plate riveted to the truck's front grill reads: "Pvt. Chambles." The cargo truck rides side-by-side with a second truck also carrying a brass band. A partially covered truck carrying military personnel follows behind.

Santa Monica's Armistice Day parade is noted in the article, "Southland Cities Ready for Patriotic Services: Parade, School Pageants and Assemblies to Feature Armistice Observances Today," Los Angeles Times, 11 Nov. 1938.

title_tesim: Canadian Legion parade at Santa Monica Blvd. and 5th St., Santa Monica, 1937
caption_tesim: Handwritten on edge of negative: 2
Text from negative sleeve: 2815. #1 to #10 inc. Canadian Legion, 7/5/37. Santa Monica Parade. City of Santa Monica.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
ark:/21198/zz002d7pj4 (document)
ark:/21198/zz002d7pkn (document)
description_tesim: Photograph of Canadian Legionnaires  marching in the seventh annual California State convention of the Canadian Legion, British Empire Service League. The men heading up the line wear light-colored pants and shirts with bow-ties, sashes across their chests, and garrison caps. Several wear medals on their chests. They are followed by flag bearers carrying both American and Union flags. Some of the flag bearers wear traditional Scottish highland attire. The procession follows street car tracks along Santa Monica Boulevard near Fifth Street, passing in front of a row of businesses. Signs on buildings and awnings read: "Office Supplies," "Dr. Campbell Credit Dentist" and "Irwin's Drugs." Further down the street is a sign advertising "Hardware." Finally, the four-story H.C. Henshey Co. department store is viewed on the far end of the boulevard (at Fourth Street).

The event was covered in a Los Times article, "Legionnaire Conclave Climaxed by Parade: Canadian Veterans March 1500 Strong Through Streets of Santa Monica," 6 Jul. 1937: A17. Also, the Bartlett collection includes related clippings from a Santa Monica newspaper. The 2-page article is titled, "Legion Parade Today Feature of Convention," 5 Jul. 1937. (see "Relation" note).

description_tesim: The Children's Home Society of California is a non-profit child welfare agency founded in 1891. It became the first licensed child placement agency in California in 1911. It was located at 919 E. 25th St. from the 1920's up to 1942, when the organization moved to the Ozro W. Childs mansion at 3100 W. Adams Blvd. where it remained until 1977.

Close-up view of the hands and skirts of girls working on a jigsaw puzzle of a map of the United States at the Children's Home Society. The print of the fabric on the skirt of the girl with freckled arms is strewn with cannons, military tents and running rabbits and monkeys holding swords.

description_tesim: Eight performers in the annual Spanish Fiesta pose in Spanish style costumes. The center woman wears a full-length dress of light colored fabric and lace with a tiered skirt. description_tesim: A succession of Ladies' Auxiliary groups photographed marching in the seventh annual California State convention of the Canadian Legion, British Empire Service League. Their banner includes a Union Jack and reads: "Hollywood Post 28 Ladies' Auxiliary." A second group follows with a banner reading: "Canadian Legion Auxiliary B.E.S.L. Post 38, Culver City, Calif." They are followed by a third Auxiliary group whose post is intelligible in this image. The procession follows street car tracks along Santa Monica Boulevard past Fourth Street, with a mission-style building seen at right. An S.H. Kress 5-10-25 Cent Store and other businesses are viewed in the background. The Guaranty Trust Bank Building (221-225 Santa Monica Blvd.) can be seen farther down the street.

The event was covered in a Los Times article, "Legionnaire Conclave Climaxed by Parade: Canadian Veterans March 1500 Strong Through Streets of Santa Monica," 6 Jul. 1937: A17. Also, the Bartlett collection includes related clippings from a Santa Monica newspaper. The 2-page article is titled, "Legion Parade Today Feature of Convention," 5 Jul. 1937. (see "Relation" note).

title_tesim: Marshall Stone mansion, "The Magnolias," Valle Vista, Hemet
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: 2809. Hemet. Marshall Stone Mansion, built 1876. Magnolias.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: Photograph of a three-story mansion called "The Magnolias." The home is brick with wood accents (including a wood plank cupola on the roof). It stands on a plot of land amidst tall trees with no other structures in view. The mansion was photographed in Valle Vista, part of the city of Hemet, in the San Jacinto Valley.

The home was owned by Marshall G. Stone, a Valle Vista pioneer considered to be the "Father of Valle Vista," having been  superintendent over the development of 3,000 acres of a San Jacinto land grant. Stone was also one of the organizers of the Fairview Land & Water Company. The house was first a hotel, and was later occupied by Stone.

title_tesim: Three women and O. H. Hewlett on Utah State Pioneer Day, Ocean Park, Santa Monica, 1938
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: 2855. 7/20/38. O. H. Hewlett. Utah Society. Mormon people.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.

description_tesim: Photograph of three women in mid-19th century pioneer dress with O. H. Hewlett, General Chairman of the Pioneer Day celebration. The two women on the right pin ribbons on his suit jacket, reading "Utah State Pioneer Day, Reception Committee, Ocean Park, July 23, 1938." They are probably members of the California Daughters of Pioneers. The woman on the left might be his wife Emily.

Pioneer Day is an official holiday celebrated in the U.S. state of Utah, with some celebrations in regions of surrounding states originally settled by Mormon pioneers. In 1938, Pioneer Day was celebrated on July 23 in Ocean Park, a neighborhood of Santa Monica. It was organized under the auspices of the society, California Daughters of Pioneers, and the presidents of five Southland Mormon stakes, and directed by General Chairman O. H. Hewlett. The day was spent in speeches, picnicking and reunion.

title_tesim: Handwritten note regarding Marshall Stone and Cecil Huntoon of Valle Vista, Hemet
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: 2809. Hemet. Marshall Stone Mansion, built 1876. Magnolias.
Handwritten text reads: Marshall Stone, 1876. Cecil Huntoon. Vallevista, Villa..., Magnolias.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: Handwritten note references Marshall Stone and Cecil Huntoon, Valle Vista pioneers. Marshall G. Stone is considered to be the "Father of Valle Vista" - having been  superintendent over the development of 3,000 acres of a San Jacinto land grant. Stone was also one of the organizers of the Fairview Land & Water Company. He built his mansion, "The Magnolias," in 1876. Cecil Huntoon is likely Joseph Cecil Huntoon (1844-), a Civil War veteran from Moline, Illinois, who settled with his family at Valle Vista in 1887. Huntoon had a son named Cecil (b. 1877 or 78).

title_tesim: Carolyn Bartlett with actor Gilbert Roland at the Will Rogers Memorial Celebration parade, Santa Monica, 1940
caption_tesim: Handwritten on edge of negative: 2901

Text from negative sleeve: 2901. Will Rogers Memorial Celebration. July 27/1940 in Santa Monica. Street Parade. Elks float. "Death Valley" Scotty. Mayor Claude Crawford, -- Johnson, Scotty's partner in group photo; Don Jackson, Carolyn Bartlett. 3: Dr Claude C Crawford U.S.C., Earl T Crawford, Barker Bros, Claude Crawford, mayor of Santa Monica.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: Related to Los Angeles Times article, “Tribute Paid Will Rogers: Five Thousand Persons and 1400 Horses March in Santa Monica Parade Santa Monica Honors Rogers Hundred Thousand See Humorist's Favorite Horse in Parade,” 28 Jul. 1940

The Will Rogers Memorial Celebration and Pageant was a three-day event with proceeds going to the Red Cross. In 1940, the event coincided with the national convention of the Will Rogers Memorial Highway (U.S. 66) Association. Festivities included a tour of Will Rogers Ranch for convention delegates, a rodeo held at Municipal Stadium, and a nightly pageant held at Santa Monica High School.

Photograph of Carolyn Bartlett with Mexican-born actor Gilbert Roland on horseback at the "Roundup of the Stars" street parade, part of the Will Rogers Memorial Celebration. Roland is seated in the saddle on a bridled horse and wears vaquero attire - a short jacket, trousers with two stripes down the side, a gun belt, and spurs on his boots. Carolyn Bartlett wears a dress and sport jacket. They are photographed on a Santa Monica street with homes on view in the background. Parade observers can also be seen across the street at left.

title_tesim: Charles B. Hervey at the entrance to El Mirasol Hotel shortly after he became the owner, Santa Barbara, 1941
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: El Mirasol Hotel, Santa Barbara. 7/10/41, C. B.  Hervey. [Crossed out:] J. B. Quigley. Miss Katherine...Rodenwald.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: Photograph of Charles B. Hervey at the entrance to El Mirasol Hotel shortly after he became the owner. He extends a hand in greeting. Hervey stands before a monumental arched doorway framed by carved acanthus leaves and with an iron grille. He is flanked by two classicizing urns topped with small terracotta flower pots.

El Mirasol began as a mansion built by Mary Herter, the mother of the artist Albert Herter. After his mother's death, Herter renovated the mansion and converted it into a boutique hotel. The hotel was purchased by Charles B. Hervey from John B. Quigley in 1941. The city block site of the former El Mirasol Hotel was dedicated as Alice Keck Park in 1980. The block is bounded by Santa Barbara, Micheltorena, Garden, and Arrellaga Streets.

title_tesim: Garden at El Mirasol Hotel, Santa Barbara, 1941
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: El Mirasol Hotel, Santa Barbara. 7/10/41, C. B.  Hervey. [Crossed out:] J. B. Quigley. Miss Katherine...Rodenwald.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: El Mirasol began as a mansion built by Mary Herter, the mother of the artist Albert Herter. After his mother's death, Herter renovated the mansion and converted it into a boutique hotel. The hotel was purchased by Charles B. Hervey from John B. Quigley in 1941. The city block site of the former El Mirasol Hotel was dedicated as Alice Keck Park in 1980. The block is bounded by Santa Barbara, Micheltorena, Garden, and Arrellaga Streets.
View through an archway towards a woman reclining in a rattan chair in a garden of El Mirasol Hotel. The garden has a lawn with walkways and cypress trees in the distance.

title_tesim: Man diving into El Mirador hotel pool, Palm Springs, 1941
caption_tesim: Text on negative sleeve: No. 2761-P410-421, El Mirador Hotel, Palm Springs, Pool - 2/1/41
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: This is a picture taken at the historic El Mirador Hotel in Palm Springs, regarded one of the finest luxury hotels of its time. This is a picture of a man diving into its famous Olympic-size swimming pool that had five diving boards, an underwater window on the pool for use by photographers, and a pool observation platform. In the background, we can see the most defining feature of the hotel, a pink Byzantine tower from which the hotel took its name — El Mirador — meaning "The Lookout."

title_tesim: Bullock's float in the Desert Circus Parade, Palm Springs, 1941
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: 2761. Palm Springs Desert Circus. 3/28/41. Mrs. Mittry. Women's Breakfast Club.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: View of the horse-drawn Bullock's float  at the Desert Circus Parade. A cowboy is at the reigns with a man in and Indian costume with a war bonnet standing at his side and a pioneer woman standing behind him. Two women in swim suits are seated along the side of the float. A painting of the Palm Springs Bulloch's shop ins on a sun shaped medallion at the back of the float. The platform of the float is covered in artificial grass with scalloped bands along the sides. Signs on the scallops include "Cahuilla Indians, Pioneer Settlers," "Mrs. Nellie Coffman Pioneer Resort Hotels," and "Bullock's Palm Springs Pioneers Resort Wear."

title_tesim: "Palm Springs, Calif., winter activities, exclusive negatives," 1936
caption_tesim: From Adelbert Bartlett1572 West Pico Blvd.Los Angeles, Calif.The Desert Riders weekly breakfast on the desert near Palm Springs; the guests ride to breakfast, which is held at sunrise, with meal prepared by cowboy cooks, and served at tables set up out in the desert. Guests of the Desert Inn shown as indicated. No. 1 The Misses Anne McMillan, Celia Deming, and Elsie McMillan, of Gross Pointe, Michigan (Detroit area), with "Chuck" Abbot, Boss of the "chuck" wagon (caterer and guide). No. 2 Mrs. Byron Harvey Jr. of Chicago, and Dr. and Mrs. Robert H. Herbst, of Chicago, relaxing after a  hearty breakfast, in the warm desert morning air. No. 3 Dr. and Mrs. Robert H. Herbst, of Chicago, on horseback, arriving at the site of the breakfast of the Desert Riders. No. 4 L to R John A.D. Lawson, of London, Eng., Anthony Burke, Chief Wrangler of The Desert Riders, Palm Springs, Calif. Guy F. Chaplin, of London, Eng.; Mrs. Judson Todd Jr., of New York City and Santa Barbara, Calif. having their orange juice in preparation for breakfast on the desert.No. 5 L to R Palm Springs Field Club -  weekly winter amateur racing meetLeft, Mrs. Freeman Gosden, wife of member of the famous Amos & Andy radio team; Miss Helen Lindsay. Long Beach, Calif., glimpsed after they had finished as competitors in a sulky race, Mrs. Gosden having won. Mr. and Mrs. Gosden are enthusiastic members of the Field Club.[Handwritten at top:] Extra copy. Other in Rock [...] File.
Text from negative sleeve: 2761 P64-P79. Palm Springs, 1936, Riding breakfasts, Desert Riders. [....] & Jeri, [...] Knowlton, trained ponies, Pacific Palisades.

description_tesim: Photograph of a still life set-up, perhaps for art class instruction, comprised of a small container holding flowers, a decanter with long spout and ornate top, and a textile draped over an art board. The composition is set on a table in front of a window. Photographed by Carolyn Bartlett, Santa Monica High School.

Date range derived from a second negative sleeve housing visually and thematically similar images. The sleeve is an envelope post marked "Apr 25, 1937". In addition, both sleeves contained photographs of a newly built Santa Monica High School structure. One photograph is of a campus construction site. The photograph documents post-earthquake reconstruction that fell under the Works Project Administration (WPA). The project was completed in 1939. title_tesim: "World Peace" float at the Annual Ocean Park Children's Floral Parade, Santa Monica, 1936
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: 2927, Ocean Park, Children's Parade, 1936
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: The event was covered in a Los Angeles Times article, "Hundreds of Children to be in Floral Parade," 27 Aug. 1936: 10.
Refer to Ark: 21198/zz002d8p09; [handwritten bottom left] La Von Merrill, Mrs. Belle, 650 Flower Ave, Peace World

The Ocean Park Annual Children's Floral Parade was a community event started in 1919. The 1936 parade was supported by the Santa Monica Bay district clubwomen and civic leaders with Mayor Gillette and the city council also lending their support. The flowers were supplied by city parks and private gardens.

Photograph of a "World Peace" float at the Seventeenth Ocean Park Children's Floral Parade. The float is decorated by flowers, with the United States Flag cut out in the shape of a shield forming the back of the float. In front, along with the sign "World Peace" is a white dove. A young girl, wearing the badge number "105" sits dressed in a long white gown, flanked by two boy scouts. Another young girl poses in front of the float. A couple (possibly parents of the children) stand behind, possibly identified as La Von Merrill and Mrs. Belle (see image: ark no.  21198/zz002d8p09).

description_tesim: The Ocean Park Annual CHildren's Floral Parade was a community event started in 1919. The Parade in 1936 was supported by the Santa Monica Bay district clubwomen and civic leaders, with Mayor Gillette and the City Council also providing their support. The Flowers were supplied by city parks and private gardens.
The event was covered in a Los Angeles Times article, "Hundreds of Children to be in Floral Parade," 27 Aug. 1936: 10.

Photograph of two young women (possibly identified as Joan Noble, Ser. Reynolds in photograph - refer to Ark) participating at the Seventeenth Annual Ocean Park Children's Floral parade. The baskets in front of their bicycles are filled with flowers. They both wear badges the read, "Children's Floral Pageant; Ocean Park, California." In the background is an arched sign, "Thank You Please Call Again and a vertical signboard for "Hotel St. Regis Apts." to the left.
Refer to Ark: 21198/zz002d8p09; [handwritten on top] bike, Joan Noble, Ser Reynolds

description_tesim: Photograph of a young woman, seated, gazing into the eyes of a male sculpture. The bust is placed atop a cabinet standing against a wall lined with artworks. The pieces appear to be influenced by advertising and graphic design. Photographed by Carolyn Bartlett, possibly during an art room open house, Santa Monica High School.

Photographs housed with this negative sleeve are related to others from a sleeve labeled "Carolyn's negatives." The photographs are visually and thematically similar. In addition, both sleeves contained photographs of Santa Monica High School structures built between 1937 and 1939 by the Works Project Administration (WPA). Both sleeves also included construction site images, likely related to the WPA’s reconstruction project (image ark nos. 21198/zz002d8j8h and 198/zz002d8k9h).

title_tesim: Photograph of Inez Schrodt and L.W. Betler enjoying a beverage at the annual Fiesta de Santa Monica, Santa Monica, 1931
caption_tesim: Caption from related typescript: Bartlett. Fiesta de Santa Monica, California [Pencil:] - old costumes. [Typewritten:] "Here's to you!" ---- member of Native Daughters of the Golden West, Mrs. Wm. Schrodt/ in costume of the "Gay Nineties" lifts a mug of the foaming beverage of a realistic hue and bubble with L.W. Betler, California Native Son, whose smiling rotundity and prominent "bay window" suggests those male aspects popular in the pre-Volstedian days, glimpsed at the "bar" set up on the Fiesta grounds (see "Relation" note).
Handwritten on edge of negative: 2669 P 12

description_tesim: Photograph of Inez Schrodt enjoying a libation with L.W. Betler at the Fiesta de Santa Monica. Schrodt wears a straw hat, blouse with gigot sleeves, chatelaine bag hanging from her belt and skirt. Schrodt was a  member of the Native daughters of the Golden West, a group dedicated to the preservation of California's history.
A related photograph with caption was published in the Woodbridge Independent, 22 May 1931: 7.

El Rey de la Vaqueros, Aug 1927

description_tesim: Eugene R. Plummer and his family once owned sections of Los Angeles including the Hancock Park area, mid-Wilshire, Hollywood Hills, and West Hollywood. The site of his home was purchased by the County of Los Angeles in 1937 and is known as Plummer Park, West Hollywood.
Eugene R. Plummer, with guitar, and unidentified woman in shawl, standing in front of Victorian-style Plummer House
Plummer House was built in Hollywood by Eugene Plummer and his brother Juan about 1874.  A portion of the house was moved to the grounds of the Leonis Adobe in Calabasas about 1982 and restored.
combined_subject_ssim: Eugene R. Plummer residence (West Hollywood, Calif.)

title_tesim: Japanese style flower arrangement by Margaret Preininger, Los Angeles, 1935
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: Miss Preininger, Flowers, Los Angeles Junior College, 1935, 2595 P 1 to 21
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: Flower arrangement in a shallow bowl with gravel at base of flowers. Margaret Preininger published a book titled Japanese flower arrangement for modern homes on December 1, 1935. At that time she was the chairman of Cultural Arts at  Los Angeles Junior College. According to the review in Kirkus Reviews, the 35 pages of text explain the four important Japanese Schools and the adaptations of their principles to American flowers and settings; the book includes 52 large reproductions of arrangements and 100 line drawings showing method of procedure; and chapters on vases, holders, flower selection, color, seasons, background and table settings

According to the review in Kirkus Reviews, the 35 pages of text explain the four important Japanese Schools and the adaptations of their principles to American flowers and settings; the book includes 52 large reproductions of arrangements and 100 line drawings showing method of procedure; and chapters on vases, holders, flower selection, color, seasons, background and table settings

title_tesim: Santa Monica College student with wreath and article about poet Ina Donna Coolbrith, Santa Monica, 1934
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: Bartlett, Santa Monica Jr. College Student, 1934, Shaw, Ina Coalbrith [sic] [should read: Coolbrith], Poetess Laureate of California
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: Young woman seated at small desk, holding book, with shell wreath mounted in frame and article, propped on desk, about Ina Donna Coolbrith, titled Sappho of the Western Sea.

The 1888 horse-shoe-shaped shell art wreath made by Ina Donna Coolbrith. The wreath was composed of sea shells interwoven with moss gathered at Santa Monica. The flowers represented include Mariposa lily, Shasta daisy, Copa de Oro, camelia, rose and other flowers. The wreath was a gift to Elizabeth Dalton Perry and William Hayes Perry. At the time of this photograph the wreath belonged to the Perrys' nephew John H. Crum of Santa Monica. The shell wreath is an example of Victorian era domestic crafts.

title_tesim: Coastal view towards road construction on a slope in the Rancho Malibu la Costa development, Malibu, circa 1927
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: View towards road construction on slope in the Rancho Malibu la Costa development, above a beach and cars on the Pacific Coast Highway. There are utility poles along the chain link fence which lines the highway on the ocean side. Bathers and a tent are visible on the beach.

Rancho Topanga Malibu Sequit was a 13,316-acre Spanish land grant given by Spanish Governor José Joaquín de Arrillaga in 1804 to José Bartolomé Tapia. In 1892 Frederick Hastings Rindge and May Knight Rindge, purchased the rancho. In 1926, the Marblehead Land Company, formed by May Knight Rindge, made a $6 million deal with Harold G. Ferguson to develop the La Costa area (between Las Flores Canyon and Carbon Canyon), but that deal fell apart when Ferguson was sentenced to jail for securities fraud in 1931. Ferguson laid out the development with street names as they remain today: Rambla Vista, Paseo Serra, etc. It took until the mid 1930's for development of the area to begin in earnest.

Dr. St. Louis Albert Estes (1876-1951) was an American doctor and the author of Raw Food and Health. He lived in Van Nuys, California in the late 1920s and early 1930s, and had 3 children with his first wife in the 1910s, 12 children with his second wife in the 1920s and 1930s.

Margaret Preininger published a book titled Japanese flower arrangement for modern homes on December 1, 1935. At that time she was the chairman of Cultural Arts at Los Angeles Junior College. According to the review in Kirkus Reviews, the 35 pages of text explain the four important Japanese Schools and the adaptations of their principles to American flowers and settings; the book includes 52 large reproductions of arrangements and 100 line drawings showing method of procedure; and chapters on vases, holders, flower selection, color, seasons, background and table settings

title_tesim: Letter from Neeta Marquis to Adelbert Bartlett, planning for story and photographs about Plummer Park, 1928
caption_tesim: Text: Dear Mr. Bartlett, I think I have finally landed that Plummer Park story---and with the Times Magazine, after all !  I took it to Mr. Criswell yesterday and told him that was the logical market for the material, and I could not see where his earlier article on the five original land grants would be at all repeated in this, even if so many months had not elapsed since the appearance of the other anyway. So He took it to examine personally, very favorably disposed toward it after I told him what personal values it represents. But he said he did not like the pictures for reproduction, and for me to ask you if you could not make a sharper print of the one showing the windmill. He would also like a lighter print of Mr. Plummer on horseback, as many of the lines would be lost in reproduction of the dark one, even if he blocks out the background, as he intends. I said I would take the matter up with you. He virtually ordered another story, too---one about the work along dramatic lines among the local Japanese, who have two schools on Noh-Drama here, it seems. I spent between three and four hours recently watching presentations of the Noh-drama at the Buddhist temple, and took copious notes. I am to-day writing to the president of their association asking for the opportunity to get fuller information. If I can arrange to get some exclusive photographs, too, will let you know, if you would like to make them. I have not been able to develop the Classical Center material yet, having many unavoidable interruptions. But the Old Mansions story has created much comment since it came out last Sunday. Some of your pictures were used, but Mr. Criswell said he sent a Times man after a few more. I shall be glad to get the Windmill story published at last, for I have spent much postage sending it around the country. My agent in New York tried it with several markets there, but said it was almost impossible to sell a feature story in New York that had not been ordered in advance. Mr. Clover said he would use the material in Saturday Night if I would make it into two shorter articles, and that he could use the horseback picture for the cover. But I didn’t want to pull the thing apart if I could place it ‘as is’, and so hope it will stay with the Times. Hoping you are all well, I remain, as ever,Sincerely yours, Neeta Marquis [signature]

title_tesim: Typewritten document describing photographs of Eugene R. Plummer and home, 1927
caption_tesim: Text: Adelbert Bartlett, 535 15th Street, Santa Monica, Calif.Eugene R. Plummer, “Colonel Plummer” as he is affectionately known to his thousands of friends, Hollywood’s oldest settler (he has never worked in motion pictures), lives on his tiny rancho and entertains after the manner of the old Spanish-Mexican dons with fiestas, music and dancing – parties wholly unlike those indulged in by the movie colony at the Brown Derby, the Ambassador, Bandstatter’s Montmarte, the Roosevelt and other hotels and cafes. Most of the original town site of Hollywood came out of Mr. Plummer’s land holdings, the Rancho La Brea, a square league of 444.4 acres, granted in 1828 by Governor Echeandia, one of the early executives of the Mexican regime in Alta California. The La Brea bitumen springs, in which the world-famous mastodons’ and saber-toothed tigers’ bones were found during the present century were part of the grant. Gradually Mr. Plummer’s acres have shrunk until his present homestead totals some five acres, but those five acres are crowded with tall eucalypti trees which afford cool shade over the horse barn and corral, the open air kitchen and dining room where fiestas are held, and the patio of his home. Annually the Vaquero Club, composed of old families, meets at Mr. Plummer’s place where the day is spent in barbeque, song, dance and general jollification after the old Spanish-Mexican style. Mr. Plummer’s father was an English Sea Captain, while his mother was Dona Cecilia Pacheco, member of an old California family. At a recent gathering of the Vaquero club Mr. Plummer’ was named “El Rey de la Vaqueros” – The King of the Vaqueros.

caption_tesim: Text reads: The large sun tower at the Carnegie Institute on the summit of Mt. Wilson in Southern California.  This tower is 150 feet above the ground.  Light from the sun is reflected from the tower's summit down to the bottom of a shaft 75 feet below the surface of the mountain top, thence up the shaft again to ground leven [sic], where daily observations and photographs of the sun are made.  Photo by Adelbert Bartlett, 535 15th Street, Santa Monica, Calif.

title_tesim: Japanese style flower arrangement with roses by Margaret Preininger, Los Angeles, 1935
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: Miss Preininger, Flowers, Los Angeles Junior College, 1935, 2595 P 1 to 21

dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: Three roses in a clear, cylindrical glass vase on a table

Margaret Preininger published a book titled Japanese flower arrangement for modern homes on December 1, 1935. At that time she was the chairman of Cultural Arts at Los Angeles Junior College. According to the review in Kirkus Reviews, the 35 pages of text explain the four important Japanese Schools and the adaptations of their principles to American flowers and settings; the book includes 52 large reproductions of arrangements and 100 line drawings showing method of procedure; and chapters on vases, holders, flower selection, color, seasons, background and table settings

title_tesim: View towards beach house under construction in the Rancho Malibu la Costa development, Malibu, circa 1927
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: Downward view from slope towards house under construction and the ocean beyond along the Pacific Coast Highway in the Rancho Malibu la Costa development on the Malibu coast.

Rancho Topanga Malibu Sequit was a 13,316-acre Spanish land grant given by Spanish Governor José Joaquín de Arrillaga in 1804 to José Bartolomé Tapia. In 1892 Frederick Hastings Rindge and May Knight Rindge, purchased the rancho. In 1926, the Marblehead Land Company, formed by May Knight Rindge, made a $6 million deal with Harold G. Ferguson to develop the La Costa area (between Las Flores Canyon and Carbon Canyon), but that deal fell apart when Ferguson was sentenced to jail for securities fraud in 1931. Ferguson laid out the development with street names as they remain today: Rambla Vista, Paseo Serra, etc. It took until the mid 1930's for development of the area to begin in earnest.

description_tesim: Text from a negative sleeve linked to a similar image appears to describe this photo as well: Long Beach, fleet from roof garden. Compo..., All Year Club, Red Island RR. 1933. [Duplicate image ark no. 21198/zz002bd3jb]

Probably photographed at the Breakers Hotel (210 E. Ocean Boulevard), that stood adjacent to the Capitol/Tracy Theatre (demolished 1974), across from the Long Beach Municipal Auditorium (now the Long Beach Convention and Entertainment Center).
This image was used in an advertisement for the All Year Club of Southern California, a Los Angeles based travel agency that promoted low cost vacations in Southern California.

Photograph of two women seated at a table on a terrace, with a girl standing apart from them at the terrace wall. A sign for the Capitol Theatre is viewed in the immediate background at left. At center ground, the Long Beach Municipal Auditorium (demolished in 1975) with a fleet of six ships in the ocean beyond.

description_tesim: This photograph was taken for an article by Neeta Marquis, "True Spirit of Art Found in City," that appeared in the Los Angeles Times on July 3, 1927

This biography from the Archives of AskART: Born in England on April 9, 1862. Cannon was educated in London and worked there as a designer for gold and silversmiths. He worked in NYC for several years before settling in Los Angeles about 1906. In 1912 he established the Cannon Art School in Los Angeles and taught there until 1933. He died in Redondo Beach, CA on Feb. 5, 1939. Member: Commercial Artists Ass'n of So. Calif.; Free Lance Art League of LA (co-founder and vice pres., 1918).
The Lyceum Theater was located at 227 S. Spring Street; it was demolished in 1941
William Henry Cannon, in his studio in the Lyceum Theater building, wearing smock, holding portrait on table, with other paintings, some nudes, some floral, on table and on wall behind

title_tesim: Typewritten note from author Neeta Marquis to Adelbert Bartlett, about photos and story about Los Angeles artists, 1927
caption_tesim: Text from nitrate negative sleeve: 2358 - 2359 - 2360 Art Studios, Los Angeles Latin Qtr, Noted Los Angeles Painters, 1927. Otis Williams, noted painter-Sculptor-Photographer with head of Boy. The Silsbys - 310 N Grand St, JM Cannon, Ruth Alexander

Text reads in part: 14 June, 1927.  Dear Mr. Bartlett, The photos are excellent beyond my expectation. I turned my story in with them to-day---made an appointment to see the Feature editor, but he had not returned at the hour he planned. Left a note for him. Miss Alexander, to whom I phoned, said they wanted to get some prints from you, if possible. I said I would let you know. Their address is 310 N. Grand, Phone Mutual 5759. Sincerely, Neeta Marquis [signature][in pencil] Sept 3 to Ruth Alexander552 E 19th St. Long Beach

title_tesim: Hanriot HD.1 fighter plane, Ocean Park Pier, Santa Monica, 1928
caption_tesim: Text from nitrate negative sleeve: 2168, Ocean Park, Nungesser Plane, 1931, Nungesser Plane
Handwritten at edge of negative: 2168 P4
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
ark:/21198/zz002bx8hz (document)
description_tesim: Hanriot HD.1 fighter plane with skull and heart emblem on fuselage, once flown by Charles Nungesser, on display on Ocean Park Pier, Santa Monica, with Patsy Mamar and Shirley Widman posing with flags in cockpit and visitors on and around benches on pier below, with ocean in background

Charles Nungesser was a successful French WWI fighter pilot, performed in air shows and movies in the United States after the war, and disappeared, with his navigator Francois Coli, during an attempted trans-Atlantic flight in 1927.
Photograph was probably taken in 1928.  Cf. uclamss_1300_1444i

Nungesser's Hanriot HD.1 was owned by Jim Granger or his widow from 1927 to 1951 (vandalized 1931 at Clover Field, Santa Monica; put in storage 1933), when it was purchased by Edward T. Maloney, restored, and put on display at the Planes of Fame Air Museum in Chino, California.

This photograph, reversed, appears in the newspaper Gobles News, Gobles, Michigan, March 22, 1928, with the caption: Hoover’s sister.  Here is Mary Van Ness Leavitt of Santa Monica, Cal., sister of Herbert Hoover, secretary of commerce and a candidate for the Republican presidential nomination.  She calls her distinguished brother “Bert.”, and in the Mason City Globe Gazette, Mason City, Iowa, March 8, 1928.

Dancers ready for starting gu[n.]  World's endurance dancing championship starting 5 pm on Washington boulevard in the motion picture studio center.  50 couples one-stepped six miles to Ocean Park amusem[ent] section where the contest will continue until completed.  Only fifty couples out of hundreds applying were accepte[d] for the contest.  Rigid physical tests were required of the entrants.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
ark:/21198/zz002bz0sp (negative)
description_tesim: Second clipping: "Marathon Dance Will Start Tomorrow at Culver City." Tomorrow at 5 p. m., 50 or more couples will start in their world's endurance dancing championship contest at Culver City.  Mayor Reve E. Houck and Mayor Herman Michel will officiate.  ... The route of the dancers will be from Culver City ... to Ocean Front and into the Bon Ton ballroom. ... The public will be permitted to dance at all times during the contest.

First clipping: "On With The Dance, Terpsichorean Marathon, Culver City to Ocean Park. With five photographs.  1. Helen Bruce, seated with feet in front of her, captioned: Pretty Helen Bruce feels rather worn and her shoes look that way after 24 hours of dancing on the highway in training for a marathon dance from Culver City to Ocean Park tomorrow.  2. Dot Harris and Stanley Whyler dancing, captioned: Dot Harris and Stanley Whyler take a hill and a turn "on high," while a friend cheers them on.  They will vie with some 50 other couples in the marathon dance of six miles.  3. Clown on stilts among crowd of dancing couples, captioned: Many champion marathon dancers from many parts of the country will compete with "local talent" from Los Angeles and the Bay district in the highway endurance dance.  This picture shows a flock of the entrants training along the beach walk at Ocean Park.  [This photograph is image uclamss_1300_1558]  4. Five shoes, soles of three visible, captioned: The dancers will "hop off" at 5 p. m. tomorrow from Culver City in the second annual World's Endurance Dancing championship and the leaders are expected to check in at the Egyptian ballroom around 9 p. m.  This photo indicates some prizes may go for new shoes.  5. Couple dancing, captioned: These dancers making a gaudy left turn are in too much of a hurry to stick out a hand.  Only experienced dancers are accepted.

title_tesim: Four photographs of Dawson, Yukon, Canada, and surroundings, circa 1899-1901, rephotographed 1958
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: Robt W Service, Dawson City, 1898, 1907, Rob Service, Poet
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: Lower right: Eldorado gold mine, Yukon.  Caption: No. 17 Eldorado, Looking up French Gulch.  Canyon crossed with mining rails, small structures, hills, and dug-out areas.

Upper right: Group gathered outside The Exchange, Dawson.  Caption: Mail Carrier Leaving Dawson for Valdez, March 22, 1901.  About 45 men posing outside building marked The Exchange, Gold Dust Bought and Sold, with dogsleds and about 8 harnessed dogs in foreground.

Upper left: Front Street, Dawson.  2 captions: 1. 70, Front Street, Dawson, Y.T., 99.  Larss & Ducl[os] Photo.  2. Front Street, Dawson, Y.T. , July 1899.  Wide dirt street with about 10 pedestrians, 3 carts, 1 dog, with wooden buildings at right, banners reading Currency, Gold Dust, Courtney Trader, Yukon Hotel, Klondike Restaurant, Eldorado, with mountains in background.

Lower left: Miners at Eldorado Creek.  2 captions: 1. Group of Miners [cut off], Larss & Duclos Photos, Dawson.  2. Group of Miners on No. 1 Eldorado Creek.  About 26 miners, most standing, most in hats, jackets, and boots, many with shovels and picks, on hillside with mining equipment in foreground and background, with dog at right.
Original photographs (upper left and lower left; possibly upper and lower right) by Per Edward Larss and Joseph E.N. Duclos.

title_tesim: Monument De Anza Marker, San Carlos Pass, 1937
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: H 2962, D'Anza Monument, San Carlos Pass, Near Hemet, 1937, By State Historical Society
Handwritten on edge of negative: 68
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: Marker has a relief image of De Anza and his men on horseback looking at mountains in the distance. The caption on the monument below the relief reads "On March 16, 1774, Juan Bautista De Anza, Indian fighter, explorer, and colonizer, led through this pass (named by him San Carlos) the first white explorers to cross the mountains into California. The party traveled from Tubac, Arizona, to Monterey, California. On December 27, 1775, on a second expedition into California, Anza led through this pass the party of Spaniards from Sonora who became the founders of San Francisco. Tablet placed by Historic Landmarks Committee, Native Sons of the Golden West. 1924".

description_tesim: Photograph is page from book, caption from photograph reads "THE ALTAR OF THE SACRED JADE PAGODA--A glorious, priceless masterpiece. 'Tis not a replica but an ideal combination of the most outstanding features of Chinese architecture. For 10 years 150 craftsmen labored to create it from a mother gem weighing 9 tons. 7 story jade pagoda, intricately carved, with four staircases and gate (pailou).

title_tesim: Clipping regarding planned blitzkrieg of Britain, 1940
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: 2964, Aviation & flying, 19th Century early fantastic baloons etc
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: This illustrated article was published in numerous U.S. newspapers between late July and mid-August of 1940.

Article titled, "British Blitkrieg Dreamed Years Ago," Evening Outlook, undated. The article discusses a sketch depicting German paratroopers invading an English port. The sketch was originally published in the German magazine, "Ueber Land und Meer" in 1895. An additional sketch illustrates a similar plan of attack devised by Napoleon in 1804 - though his plot  involved creating a tunnel under the English channel through which soldiers would march.

title_tesim: Paintings in an American naive style by Jane Wooster Scott at the Windemere Hotel, Santa Monica, 1954
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: John Lindsay...Lady Windemere. 8/5/54.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: The Hotel Windemere was located at 1431 Ocean Ave. Built in 1909, it was demolished in 1962. John Lindsay, a southern California architect, owned the building from at least 1954 until its demolition. According to newspaper reports from 1954 to 1956, he and Jane Wooster Scott were a couple.

Photograph of 2 paintings in an American naïve style of a landscape with musicians in a gazebo (left) and a snowscape with a church and houses. Signed "Wooster," the paintings are early works by Jane Wooster Scott (b. 1929). The paintings hang on a wall covered in striped wall paper at the Windemere Hotel.

title_tesim: "Hansel and Gretel" production with Barbara Ludwig and Charlie Marie Gordon (possibly), Barnum Hall, Santa Monica, 1959?
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: 1957-59. Hansel and Gretel.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: Santa Monica Civic Opera production of "Hansel and Gretel." Performers in the role of Hansel (possibly portrayed by Charlie Marie Gordon), Gretel (Barbara Ludwig), and their fellow cast members wear German-style clothing - females portraying young males wear lederhosen; the young women wear dirndls. Behind them, a stage set depicts a gingerbread cottage in the woods. The location is most likely Barnum Hall.

Probably related to a production of "Hansel and Gretel," January 23-24, 1959. The performance was conducted by Irving Beckman and performed at Barnum Hall. The notice appears in the Los Angeles Times, 18 Jan. 1959: E6

title_tesim: "Hansel and Gretel" production with June Moss and one other performer, Barnum Hall, Santa Monica, 1957-1959

description_tesim: A notice for a Santa Monica Civic Opera production of "Hansel and Gretel," conducted by Irving Beckman and performed at Barnum Hall, appears in the Los Angeles Times, 18 Jan. 1959: E6

Santa Monica Civic Opera production of "Hansel and Gretel." June Moss (as Hansel and Gretel's mother), and an unidentified singer as their father, perform at stage left. They are dressed as German peasants and stand within a cottage interior set. Two and three-dimensional trees depict a woodland exterior. View of stage and orchestra pit from balcony. Performed at Barnum Hall, Santa Monica.

title_tesim: Pearl Jennings from "O.K. By Me" directed by Louise Glaum, Los Angeles, 1952
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: Louise Glaum, 1952, O.K. by Me, Play, Louise Glaum.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: This production was referenced by two Los Angeles Times articles, "Stage Farce Offered at Beaux Arts Theater." 11 Oct. 1952, and "Beaux Arts Will Reopen." 19 Sep. 1952.

Louise Glaum was the director of this production of "O.K. By Me," a play by Sheldon Shepard. Mrs. Glaum was an actress, drama teacher and director, who was active in the Los Angeles area for over 60 years. Originally known as one of the first "vamp" actresses, she later opened several theaters in Los Angeles and an acting school.

This image is of cast member Pearl Jennings who played the role of Belle, the maid, in the production of the Sheldon Sheppard play "O.K. By Me." Jennings is wearing a dark dress, necklace, earrings, and bracelet.

title_tesim: Roof of the Sorrento Beach Club, Santa Monica, 1952
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: Mr. Philbin, Sorrento Beach Club 1952, S.M. Pool.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: P. H. Philbin, of Chicago, a former owner of the Sorrento Beach Club building, had planned a 12-story apartment building on the site in 1953. The project included utilizing the massive concrete Sorrento Beach Club structure which had been abandoned in an unfinished state. The project was controversial, as some property owners felt that it would mar the beauty of the Santa Monica Palisades Park.
This is an image of the roof of the Sorrento Beach Club as seen from the Palisades Park cliffs above, looking south. Highway 1, houses, another large building, and the beach can also be seen.

title_tesim: Typewritten description of photographed patchwork quilt, 1934
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: 2645 P1 to 3, 10/10/34. Welfare Center sewing room. SM 1934. Marie Finley left. Betty Shoemaker (Right) with blanket.

ark:/21198/zz002d459x (print)
description_tesim: Photograph of a typescript document describing a collection of 100-year-old patchwork quilts donated to the Santa Monica municipal welfare center sewing room in Santa Monica. Workers, Marie Finley and Betty Shoemaker are also identified.

The Santa Monica welfare center appears to be affiliated with the Santa Monica Welfare Council. A solicitation for clothing donations (referencing both “the Santa Monica welfare center” and “the council”) appeared in the Santa Monica Evening Outlook article, “Welfare Council Wants Clothes,” 3 Jun. 1927:1.

title_tesim: “Lucia di Lammermoor” production with Betty Herrick and Giovanni Zavatti, John Adams Auditorium, Santa Monica, 1951
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: 1951. Lucia. Opera. Civic Opera. Betty Herrick. Natalie & Kay Mazet.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: Betty Herrick as Lucia and Giovanni Zavatti as Edgardo in “Lucia di Lammermoor.” Herrick wears a gown emulating 17th century attire. It is comprised of a full skirt, lace overlay bodice with long sleeves, and a tie around the waist. An organza shawl and bejeweled necklace and earrings complete the ensemble. Zavatti wears a jerkin, short full breeches, cavalier boots and a baldric with sword. They stand in front of a flat background with a painted fountain and exterior architecture representing Lammermoor Castle. The dimly lit stage suggests nighttime. The conductor’s podium and musical scores can be seen from the orchestra pit at foreground. Likely performed at John Adams Junior High School, Santa Monica.

description_tesim: Betty Herrick in the title role of Lucia, from the Santa Monica Civic Opera production, “Lucia di Lammermoor.” Herrick, wearing a 17th century inspired satin robe and smock and holding a bouquet of roses, is photographed presumably during her curtain call. A man with a camera stands offstage to her left. Likely photographed at John Adams Junior High School, Santa Monica.

title_tesim: Sorrento Beach Club, Santa Monica, 1952
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: Mr. Philbin, Sorrento Beach Club 1952, S.M. Pool
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections

dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: This is an image of the Sorrento Beach Club from the Palisade Park cliffs above. Highway 1, houses, and the beach can also be seen.

P. H. Philbin, of Chicago, a former owner of the Sorrento Beach Club building, had planned a 12-story apartment building on the site in 1953. The project included utilizing the massive concrete Sorrento Beach Club structure which had been abandoned in an unfinished state. The project was controversial, as some property owners felt that it would mar the beauty of the Santa Monica Palisades Park.

description_tesim: This image is of the captured Japanese submarine mentioned in the L.A. Times article from Dec. 17, 1941.

This image is of a crowd of onlookers viewing a recovered Japanese submarine. Several people stand up on a platform next to the submarine and look in its portholes. Other people mill around in front of the submarine, including a soldier in uniform. In the far background is a sign on a building that reads “Altman’s Fine Furniture Moderately Priced 1344 Fourth Street.” The hood of a car is inthe foreground.

description_tesim: This image is of Monty (Plummer H.) Montgomery, a lapidarist who worked and lived in Santa Monica. He stands holding a sphere of stone, with a small hammer and larger stones before him on a table. Behind him is a shelving unit with shelves full of stones carved into spheres.  To his right are several cases of stone specimens carved into various shapes such as: hearts, stars, and ovals. He is referenced as a lapidary in a July 18, 1945 L.A. Times article.

title_tesim: Typescript description of a photograph of a midget Japanese submarine, 1943
caption_tesim: Photograph by Adelbert Bartlett, Santa Monica, Calif. 2/10/1943. "Please to shoot me intimately," begged one of the two surviving Japanese crewmen of this midget submarine when it surrendered to American forces after they had literally bombed the deadly little craft out of the Pacific and up onto the beach near Honolulu on Dec. 7, 1941. The Yanks declined to accommodate him, but captured the sub intact and brought it to the States. A shadowy "ax" for the Axis, grasped by a hand lifted above a steel helmeted head mysteriously appeared on an automobile hood in foreground as the photographer snapped this picture at Santa Monica, California, where the sub was shown on a nationwide tour to boost the sale of war bonds.

title_tesim: Newspaper sketch showing the course of the 13-mile San Jacinto tunnel, photographed 1941
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: 2761. 1000 Palms. 1/15/41. Paul P. Wilhelm (portraits). Also composites for postcards.

Text from newspaper caption: Sketch showing course of 13-mile San Jacinto tunnel which nears completion. Hard-rock miners have been working on the project, a link in the 392-mile Colorado River Aqueduct of the Metropolitan Water District, three years. At lower left is an artist map of the entire system. [Los Angeles Times, 16 Nov. 1938: A1]

description_tesim: This negative is grouped with negatives of  1000 Palms Ranch, a desert oasis near Thousand Palms. Paul P. Wilhelm inherited the  property from his father, lived there from 1935-1993, and operated it as a desert resort. He housed San Jacinto Tunnel workers during the three-year construction project. The property is now the Thousand Palms Oasis Preserve in the Coachella Valley Reserve.

title_tesim: Santa Monica Beach and Yacht Harbor from Palisades Park, Santa Monica, circa 1946
caption_tesim: Handwritten on edge of negative: 2824 P 35
Text from negative sleeve: 2824 P31 to 35. Santa Monica. Views from Jones Monument to yacht harbor. See also file #2600.  Show complete file #.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
subject_tesim: Santa Monica State Beach Park (Santa Monica, Calif.)
description_tesim: At some point after WWII the rustic branch fence at Palisades park was replaced with two types: a pre-cast concrete fence with a criss-cross design and a wood board fence. Bartlett photographs of Palisades Park dated 1946 show the rustic branch fence and the wood rail fence.

Photograph of a young woman standing at a rustic eucalyptus branch fence overlooking the Santa Monica Bay. The path follows a stretch of beach to the Yacht Harbor and Pier, with boats viewed at right. The image is framed by a large palm tree at foreground. Photographed near John P. Jones Monument, Palisades Park.

John P. Jones Monument (located within Palisades Park at Ocean Avenue and Wilshire Boulevard) is dedicated to Nevada Senator John P. Jones, co-founder of the town of Santa Monica with Colonel Robert S. Baker, 1875. Coordinates derived from this location.

This photo (labeled 2824 P 35) may have been taken around the same time as nine photograph negatives of Palisades Park and Santa Monica Beach labeled “2824 P 20 to 30.” Text from associated negative sleeve: 2824 P 20 to 30. Santa Monica, From Palisades Park. 1946. Wide angle. Show full file #.
combined_subject_ssim: Santa Monica State Beach Park (Santa Monica, Calif.)

dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: The celebration of Navy Day is referenced in the L.A. Times article from October 25, 1934.

This image is of a group of people, probably standing on the ship the U.S. (or S.S.?) Philip. Numerous sailors stand dressed in naval uniforms, while a group of civilians looks on, wearing day clothes. William Carter, Mayor of Santa Monica, is in the center. Behind them, the tower of the ship can be seen.

title_tesim: Fishing at the pier, Santa Monica, 1934
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: 2617 P1 to 3. Navy Day, Santa Monica. U.S. Philip, Oct 27/34. Mayor Carter.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: Photograph of mostly women and children fishing from a dock in the Santa Monica Bay. A small boat sits on the platform at foreground, with a young boy and girl standing inside. An older boy leans against the bow of the boat and looks toward the camera, as does another boy seen with a friend at the stern - their fishing pole resting against the boat. Others in the photo hold fishing poles and direct their gaze toward the water. Along the distant beach stands several structures that include the castle-style Deauville Club. Probably photographed on Navy Day, October 27, 1934.
